---
name: mapps
description: Set up the multi-repo workspace and build project maps. Runs mapps init to clone repos from repos.list, then explores every app under apps/ that has no CLAUDE.md and writes CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md maps. Use when the user asks to set up the workspace, add repositories, or build/rebuild project maps.
argument-hint: [git-url ...]
allowed-tools: Bash(mapps *)
---

<!-- Generated by mapps — regenerated on every `mapps init`. Do not edit. -->

Work from the workspace root: the directory that holds `repos.list` (or where it should be created).

## Output location

Write any generated artifacts — a new project scaffolded from the apps, a security-audit report over all apps, anything else the user asks you to produce — under `results/`, never into `apps/` or the workspace root.

## Step 1 — run the CLI

- Check the binary exists: `command -v mapps`. If missing, tell the user to run `go install github.com/rus-lan/multiApps/cmd/mapps@latest` and stop.
- Run `mapps init $ARGUMENTS` (any git URLs passed to this command go straight through). This creates `repos.list` if needed, clones missing repos into `apps/`, and regenerates the root `Makefile`.
- If it exits non-zero because some repos failed to clone, report which ones failed and why, then continue with the repos that do exist.

## Step 2 — find apps without maps

List the directories under `apps/`. For each `apps/<dir>`:

- `CLAUDE.md` missing → the app needs a map (Step 3).
- `CLAUDE.md` present but `AGENTS.md` missing → copy `CLAUDE.md` to `AGENTS.md` (they must be identical).

If nothing needs work, say all maps exist and stop.

## Step 3 — explore and write maps

For each app that needs a map — parallel subagents are allowed, one per app:

Explore the repo (README, package manifests, entry points, directory layout, configs, CI files) and write `apps/<dir>/CLAUDE.md` in English with exactly this structure:

1. **Purpose** — 1–2 sentences on what the app does.
2. **Stack and entry points** — language, framework, main files.
3. **Key directories** — short annotated list.
4. **How to build / run / test** — must agree with the root Makefile targets: `make build-<dir>`, `make run-<dir>`, `make test-<dir>`. If the generated commands are wrong, state the correct ones here and fix the Makefile via Step 5.
5. **External dependencies** — databases, queues, required env vars, config files.
6. **Notable gotchas** — only if you found real ones; drop the section otherwise.

Then copy the file so both exist and are identical:

```
cp apps/<dir>/CLAUDE.md apps/<dir>/AGENTS.md
```

Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md; opencode reads AGENTS.md. Both files are listed in each clone's `.git/info/exclude` by mapps, so they never reach the app's remote.

## Step 4 — update the root index

Create or update the workspace root `CLAUDE.md`: one line per app — dir, stack, one-sentence purpose. Keep existing entries for apps whose maps were already there; add or refresh entries for the maps you just wrote.

## Step 5 — fix wrong Makefile commands (only when needed)

Never edit the root `Makefile` — it is regenerated. Put corrections in `mk/<dir>.mk` by setting the command variables (in the variable name, replace every character that is not a letter, digit, or `_` with `_`):

```make
BUILD_<var> = <correct build command>
RUN_<var> = <correct run command>
TEST_<var> = <correct test command>
```

The commands run from inside `apps/<dir>`.

## Step 6 — report

Summarize: repos cloned / skipped / failed, maps written, root index updated, `mk/` overrides added.
